scope_note: Note 1: Headings 55.01 and 55.02 apply only to man-made filament TOW meeting ALL specifications: (a) length >2m; (b) twist <5 turns/m; (c) per filament <67 decitex; (d) for synthetic tow only: must be DRAWN (cannot be stretched >100% of its length); (e) total measurement >20,000 decitex. Tow ≤2m length → classified in 55.03 or 55.04 (staple fibres).

inclusions.description: Synthetic filament tow (polyester, nylon, acrylic, polypropylene etc.) meeting Note 1 specifications: length >2m, twist <5 turns/m, per filament <67 dtex, drawn (not stretchable >100%), total >20,000 dtex
Artificial filament tow (viscose/rayon, lyocell, cupro, modal, acetate etc.) meeting Note 1 specifications: length >2m, twist <5 turns/m, per filament <67 dtex, total >20,000 dtex
Synthetic staple fibres, not carded, combed or otherwise processed for spinning (polyester, nylon/PA, acrylic/PAN, polypropylene staple — cut or broken from filaments or tow ≤2m)
Artificial staple fibres, not carded, combed or otherwise processed for spinning (viscose/rayon, cupro, modal, lyocell, acetate staple fibres)
Waste (including noils, yarn waste and garnetted stock) of man-made fibres (synthetic or artificial)
Synthetic staple fibres, carded, combed or otherwise processed for spinning
Artificial staple fibres, carded, combed or otherwise processed for spinning
Sewing thread of man-made staple fibres, whether or not put up for retail sale
Other yarn of synthetic staple fibres, not put up for retail sale
Other yarn of artificial staple fibres, not put up for retail sale
Yarn of man-made staple fibres, put up for retail sale
Woven fabrics of synthetic staple fibres, containing 85% or more by weight of synthetic staple fibres (majority-synthetic wovens)
Woven fabrics of synthetic staple fibres, containing less than 85% by weight of such fibres, mixed mainly or solely with cotton, of a weight not exceeding 170 g/m2
Woven fabrics of synthetic staple fibres, containing less than 85% by weight of such fibres, mixed mainly or solely with cotton, of a weight exceeding 170 g/m2
Other woven fabrics of synthetic staple fibres (mixed with fibres other than cotton — e.g., with wool, flax, silk)
Woven fabrics of artificial staple fibres

exclusions.condition: Man-made filament tow NOT meeting Note 1 specifications (length ≤2m, or twist ≥5 turns/m, or per filament ≥67 dtex, or not drawn for synthetic)
exclusions.action: Tow ≤2m length → classified as STAPLE FIBRES in 55.03 (synthetic) or 55.04 (artificial), not 55.01/55.02. Tow not meeting other specs → classify by its physical characteristics in the appropriate heading

exclusions.condition: Man-made filaments in continuous form (filament yarn, monofilament, multifilament — not staple)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 54 (man-made filaments) — Ch.55 covers only STAPLE FIBRES (cut or broken from continuous filaments). Continuous filament yarn → Ch.54

exclusions.condition: Woven fabrics of man-made staple fibres that are impregnated, coated, covered or laminated
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 59 (impregnated, coated textile fabrics) — coated wovens of man-made staple → Ch.59 (59.03 if plastics coated; 59.06 if rubber coated; 59.07 if otherwise coated)

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Tow vs. staple fibre distinction: the 2m length and specification tests
classification_rules.description: The key distinction between filament tow (55.01/55.02) and staple fibres (55.03/55.04) is the LENGTH and technical specifications: TOW (55.01/55.02) = parallel filaments of uniform length, with the bundle exceeding 2m, low twist (<5 turns/m), thin filaments (<67 dtex each). SYNTHETIC tow (55.01) additionally must be DRAWN (cannot be stretched >100% — i.e., already oriented). Tow ≤2m → STAPLE FIBRES (55.03 or 55.04). Tow meeting all specs but not drawn (synthetic) → stays as tow stock but classified as staple if ≤2m.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.55 (man-made STAPLE fibres) vs Ch.54 (man-made FILAMENT yarns and fibres)
classification_rules.description: The staple/filament split is the primary division for man-made textile fibres: Ch.54 = man-made fibres in CONTINUOUS FILAMENT form (filament yarn, monofilament strips, multifilament yarn — not cut or broken). Ch.55 = man-made fibres CUT or BROKEN into discrete lengths (staple fibres) and all downstream products therefrom. A polypropylene monofilament strip (54.04/54.05) vs. polypropylene staple (55.03). Viscose continuous filament yarn (54.03) vs. viscose staple fibre (55.04).

classification_rules.rule: Processing stage chain for man-made staple: raw fibre → carded → yarn → fabric
classification_rules.description: Ch.55 spans the full production chain of man-made STAPLE products: (1) RAW STAPLE: 55.03 (synthetic) / 55.04 (artificial) — uncarded, unprocessed. (2) WASTE: 55.05. (3) CARDED/COMBED: 55.06 (synthetic) / 55.07 (artificial). (4) YARN: 55.08 (sewing thread), 55.09 (synthetic yarn, not retail), 55.10 (artificial yarn, not retail), 55.11 (retail sale yarn, both types). (5) WOVEN FABRICS: 55.12–55.16 (synthetic wovens by composition %; artificial wovens). Each stage moves to a higher heading; the processing stage determines which heading applies.

classification_rules.rule: Woven fabric headings (55.12–55.16): composition × blend × weight matrix for synthetic staple fabrics
classification_rules.description: Synthetic staple woven fabrics (55.12–55.15) classified by COMPOSITION and WEIGHT: 55.12 = synthetic staple fabrics ≥85% synthetic — straightforward majority-synthetic. <85% synthetic: 55.13 = mixed mainly/solely with COTTON, weight ≤170 g/m². 55.14 = mixed mainly/solely with COTTON, weight >170 g/m². 55.15 = mixed with OTHER fibres (wool, linen, silk — not mainly cotton). Artificial staple woven fabrics → ALL in 55.16 regardless of blend or weight. Blends with both cotton and other fibres → determine the principal secondary fibre ('mixed mainly or solely').

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 55.08 (sewing thread) vs 55.09/55.10 (other yarn not for retail) vs 55.11 (yarn for retail sale)
classification_rules.description: Man-made staple yarn headings split by END USE and PRESENTATION: 55.08 = SEWING THREAD of man-made staple (both synthetic and artificial), whether or not for retail. 55.09 = other SYNTHETIC staple yarn NOT put up for retail (industrial bobbins, cones for manufacturing). 55.10 = other ARTIFICIAL staple yarn NOT put up for retail. 55.11 = yarn of man-made staple fibres (both synthetic and artificial) PUT UP FOR RETAIL SALE (smaller bobbins/balls/skeins for domestic use). The retail/non-retail distinction is based on presentation form and quantity, not yarn count.
